title: How should CUDA execution-space qualifiers display?
summary: CUDA qualifiers such as __global__, __device__, and __host__ are currently source-verbatim and seafoam, while a compact semantic vocabulary has not yet been selected.
details: The qualifiers carry execution semantics and should not disappear through a generic underscore-conceal rule.
A leading candidate is __global__ -> kernel, __device__ -> device, and __host__ -> host, which keeps __host__ __device__ visibly dual-target as host device.
A more mechanical alternative removes only the double-underscore fences and displays global, device, and host.
Related qualifiers need an intentional family policy: __shared__, __constant__, __managed__, __restrict__, __forceinline__, __noinline__, and __launch_bounds__ do not all describe execution space.
Any choice must work in raw CUDA declarations, structured function rendering, alignment width calculation, cursor/Visual reveal, module toggles, and actual ext_linegrid output.
The existing pinned NVIDIA CUDA Samples scene supplies a real __global__ kernel declaration for a style-lab comparison.

provenance.note: Owner asked whether __global__, __device__, and related qualifiers should be mangled in some way but did not yet select a spelling.
The current frontend colors these source spellings as CUDA but does not conceal or replace them.

evidence.claim: A revision-pinned real kernel declaration is available for comparing qualifier spellings through Neovim's ext_linegrid.

questions: Which CUDA qualifier spelling decision is still open?

last_verified.method: Current highlight/conceal paths inspected and owner uncertainty preserved as an open style-lab question.
